observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and Management agreed to the following:
The HRMO, during budget preparations of the Annual Budget, to include not only the retirable/co-terminous employees and elected officials during the Annual Budget period but also those employees/officials who will retire and whose term of service will end in the ensuing year in the computation of the terminal leave benefits;
The Local Finance Committee (LFC) to prioritize the allocation of leave benefits computed by the HRMO in the Annual Budget; and
The MA to:
Recognize in the books of accounts the unrecorded leave benefits payable and subsequently, the leave benefits which are expected to be settled within 12 months after the end of the reporting period, subject to certification of appropriation and available funds from the MBO and Municipal Treasurer. The proposed adjusting entries are as follows:
Prior Period Adjustments			P 5.921 million
Leave Benefits Payable			P 5.921 million
To recognize prior years accumulated earned leave credits
as of December 31, 2024.
Suggested accounting entries upon payment:
Leave Benefits Payable
Terminal Leave Benefits*
Prior Period Adjustments**
Cash in Bank
To record payment of accumulated earned leave credits
*Leave credits earned during CY 2025
** PYs’ leave benefits due to increase of salary in CY 2025;
Ensure compliance with IPSAS 1 and IPSAS 20 by providing the required disclosures in the NFS, specifically on the aggregate renumeration of key management and those who are already retired for the year and those that are retiring in the next reporting period, as well as the Municipality’s adoption of IPSAS 39 in accordance with COA Circular No. 2024-009; and
Provide disclosure in the NFS on the nature of the leave benefits payable and its expected payment for at least the following year.

We recommended, and Management agreed to the following:
The MTO to
Prepare the certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year, based on the RPTARs it maintains, and submit the same to the MAO to facilitate the setting-up of collectibles in the books; and
Prepare the schedule of RPT/SET receivables showing the tax delinquencies of each taxpayer as of year-end.
The MAO to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the establishment of RPT/SET Receivable and Deferred RPT/SET account balances only after receiving the MTO’s certified list of taxpayers and adjust the same based on the Abstract of Collections.

We recommended, and the management agreed to ensure the compliance of the LGU with the policies and procedures on Local Roads Asset Management System by requiring the:
The MGSO to:
Coordinate with the ME, and develop a comprehensive Road Map to accurately determine all roads owned by the Municipality;
Submit a Report on Local Road Network at the end of the accounting period using the prescribed format in Annex A of COA Circular 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
The ME to prepare and maintain a Report on Road Networks of the LGU with complete description and cost segregation of road components for all road projects of the Municipality as of reporting period, and provide the same to the MA and MGSO.
The MA:
Based on the Report on Road Networks from the ME, to update the records on road network to properly recognize the cost-segregation of road components;
To prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Card as subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
To provide proper disclosure of the road network system in the NFS.

We recommended, and Management agreed to the following:
The Local Chief Executive (LCE) to create an IC composed of adequate number of members and temporarily relieve them of all their regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is completed in three months or less;
The IC:
In coordination with the MGSO, to carefully plan/strategize on how to complete the physical inventory and revise the PIP by providing a detailed schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory taking activities from start up to its targeted completion; the PPE items to be counted per schedule; and the cut-off date and submit the same to the COA Audit Team;
To record/document the physical count daily using the ICF and state clearly the actual condition of the PPEs as provided in Paragraph 6.2.6 of the Circular; and
Upon completion of the physical count, to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network, for Road Networks, as shown in Annex C of COA Circular 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 and RPCPPE, for other PPE accounts, using the prescribed format as provided in Annex 49 of Volume II of NGAS for LGUs.
The MGSO:
To require each office to fill out the ICF (Annex A of the Circular) to be the list of existing PPE under their custody to be submitted to the GSO for verification prior to the conduct of the actual inventory by the IC.
In coordination with the IC, to:
Completely adopt the uniform property identification system wherein a unique Property Number shall be assigned for each PPE item of all PPE (except Road Networks) accounts of the LGU following the required code format in the numbering system as provided in Paragraph 5.6 of the Circular; and
Ensure that all PPE items counted shall be tagged with the new Property Stickers containing all vital information of the PPE provided under Paragraph 5.7 of the Circular.
The MA and MGSO agreed to undertake the reconciliation process by adhering to the procedures provided in Sections 6.3.1 and 6.3.2 and shall be completed within 10 days from rendition of the RPCPPE by the IC.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to establish control mechanisms to ensure the proper coordination of its Office with the MGSO on the maintenance of records for fuel consumption.
We further recommended, and the MGSO agreed to assume responsibility for the monitoring of fuel usage by ensuring the timely submission and preparation of essential documents and records which include the RIV, Daily Gas Issue Record, Summary of Daily Totals of RIV, Gasoline/Fuel Control Cards, and the FCR, to ensure proper monitoring and reporting in compliance with COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977.

We recommended, and the management agreed to the following:
The LCE to issue internal memoranda clearly outlining the roles and responsibilities of each office/end-user in relation to documentation, report preparation, and financial recording, which aims to ensure consistency and accountability in documenting Welfare Goods Expenses and to strengthen compliance with financial reporting standards.
The MA to establish mandatory guidelines governing the distribution of welfare goods including the submission of SSMI with distribution lists, photo documentation, and accomplishment/terminal reports to ensure evidence that goods reach to the intended beneficiaries and will support the proper recording of Welfare Goods Expense.
The MGSO to monitor the status of distribution of welfare goods, and actively follow-up on the submission of distribution list from end-users.

Moving forward, we recommended, and Management agreed to:
Exercise prudence in the disbursement of the LDRRMF and immediately stop the practice of charging expenses that are not directly aligned to disaster response and emergency operations;
Consider increasing the MOOE allocation under the GF for the MDRRMO to cover administrative and operational costs; and
Provide a separate allocation in the GF for insurance coverage of the LGU properties.

We recommended, and the LSB agreed to:
Ensure that all quarterly and annual reports are submitted to the DepEd CO and copies be furnished to the Local Sanggunian, and the RO of the DBM IX and DILG IX not later than 20th day after the end of the quarter and for the annual report not later than the 15th day of February of the following year; and
Designate a compliance officer within the LSB to track and verify submission, ensuring that all required agencies receive copies on or before the given schedule.

We recommended, and the GFPS agreed that in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to:
Update the GAD database by conducting gender analysis to identify gender issues/gaps and determine gender-responsiveness PPAs for prioritization in programming and budgeting in the annual GPB;
Ensure that proposed GAD PPAs of departments for allocation are supported by program profile containing the identified gender issues/GAD mandates and gender analysis situation of the community; and
Attend refresher training on gender analysis, GAD mainstreaming, and effective utilization of GAD data.

We recommended, and the members of the Municipality Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) headed by the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
Intensify the implementation of waste segregation at the source by requiring both households and business establishments to properly separate biodegradable and non-biodegradable waste through regular information, education, and communication (IEC) campaigns conducted at the barangay level, particularly during scheduled barangay assemblies.
Strictly enforce the penalties and sanctions stipulated under Municipal Ordinance No. 009-009-2019 for failure to segregate waste and for violations related to improper waste disposal.
Conduct regular and systematic inspections at the Central MRF to ensure that collected waste is properly segregated and handled in accordance with established procedures. This will aid in monitoring compliance and help detect issues early.
Prioritize the hiring and deployment of additional job orders assigned to handle the sorting and processing of waste to reduce the backlog and prevent further accumulation of unsorted waste.

We recommended, and Management agreed to consider allocating a budget for the purchase of garbage truck with compartment and/or devise the vehicle, whichever is practicable, to ensure that it is fully covered to contain wastes during collection and transportation.

We recommended, and Management agreed to:
Provide regular supplies of PPE to solid waste management personnel directly dealing with the handling of solid waste while taking into consideration their comfortability while using the PPE by annually allocating a separate appropriation for MOOE-PPE under the Office of MENR for the procurement of PPE to ensure long-term support for solid waste workers;
Invite personnel from DENR-EMB to conduct training of solid waste and include training for occupational health and safety procedures; and
Regularly conduct periodical medical tests and provide free medical treatment in cases of unfortunate events occurring during garbage collection and transport which aligns with the law’s overarching goal of ensuring that waste is handled in a manner that protects both public health and the well-being of those directly involved in waste collection and management.

We reiterated our recommendation, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
Municipal Treasurer to take the necessary measures to update the RPTAR and Taxpayer’s Index Card, respectively, in order to come-up with a schedule of RPT and SET Receivables to facilitate recording in the books.
Municipal Accountant to record/book-up at the beginning of the year the RPT and SET Receivables and its corresponding Deferred RPT/SET based on the Certified List of Taxpayers/RPTAR provided by the Municipal Treasurer and adjust the same based on the Abstract of Collections.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Send written notices to the payees to confirm the existence of the stale checks and advise the payees to submit the stale check for replacement following the procedure prescribed in Section 59 of Chapter 3 of the MNGAS for LGUs Volume I;
Regularly monitor unclaimed checks and practice the sending of written notice to the payee/s at least one month before the check becomes stale; and
Regularly submit a List of Stale Checks to the Municipal Accountant for proper action in the books of accounts.
Moreover, we also recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to review the list of outstanding checks reflected in the monthly BRS and, if found to be stale and upon receipt of the List of Stale Checks from the Municipal Treasurer, make any necessary adjustments in the LGU's books.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/Deficit, reflects the correct amount of reconciling items with supporting schedules, to reconcile with the cash from operating activities presented in the Statement of Cash Flows represents the LGU's correct, true, and factual operating cash as of December 31, 2023, moving forward.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Engineer to:
Bill and collect the prescribed rates as embodied in the Local Revenue Code to enable the LGU earn substantial amount of income that could be useful for betterment of the water works services.
Find ways and means of improving water services so that all consumers could enjoy the same privilege of always having sufficient supply of water.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the BAC to:
Scout for more Non-Government Organization (NGOs) and peoples Organization (POs) that could be tapped/invited as observers in future procurement projects of the LGU.
Ensure that invited observers possessed relevant knowledge, experience and expertise to a particular procurement.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the BAC to ensure that for future procurements requiring the conduct of pre-procurement,  all officials involved in the procurement meet and discuss all aspects of a specific procurement activity, which includes the technical specifications, the ABC, the applicability and appropriateness of the recommended method of procurement and the related milestones, the bidding documents, and availability of the pertinent budget release for the project.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the BAC to strictly require the BAC-TWG to conduct and exercise utmost prudence in the post-qualification of the S/LCB in order to properly determine whether the S/LCB complies with all the eligibility requirements and conditions specified in the RIRR, of RA 9184.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
End-users to prepare PRs with complete technical specifications of goods requested.
BAC examine and ensure that PRs process for procurement shall contain complete technical specifications of the products/items to be procured to afford reasonable basis in determining the most advantageous price for the government.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Notify the suppliers/contractors of their long outstanding retention money and require them to submit copy of previously received Disbursement Voucher and other supporting documents showing the retention money deducted from their last payment.
Return the retention money of the suppliers after the lapse of the warranty period to avoid further incurrence of penalty upon receipt of the letter request from the suppliers and its supporting documents.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the BAC to promptly submit advance copy (ies) of POs and contracts together with the prescribed supporting documents required under COA Circular 2009-001 and 2023-004 to the Audit Team to facilitate timely technical review and evaluations thereof.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the following personnel to:
Municipal Budget Officer look into this concern by appropriating funds for the insurance of its properties and assets
The Municipal Treasurer facilitate processing of insurance coverage with GSIS for Building and Structures.
The Municipal Accountant record the LTO Registration expenses under Taxes and License account instead of Insurance expenses to properly present the accounts in the FS.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to:
Direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to endorse to the Committee on Family, Women, Children, and Elderly of the SB the passing of an ordinance localizing RA 11313 and post the same in conspicuous places; and
Include in the ordinance provisions on the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof; and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We recommended, and the GFPS agreed to formulate a GAD Agenda for the remaining term of the LCE in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and be made as basis in the preparation of the annual GPB.

We reiterated our recommendation, and Management agreed to create and establish Monitoring and Evaluation Team in consonance with the provision Section 5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to evaluate the LGU GAD policies, programs and projects.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to require the GFPS in coordination with the MPDC to:
Update the GAD Database to contain gender analysis identifying gender issues and determine gender-responsive PPAs for prioritization in programming and budgeting in the annual GPB in accordance with Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No.2013-01; and
Prepare the annual GPB in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No.2013-01, as amended by JMC No.2016-01 and include priority gender issues identified in the GAD Database.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to to direct the Municipal Engineer to provide Emergency Exit in the 2nd floor of both buildings including the appropriate stairs/ladder for ensure the safety of building occupants in case of emergency, and allocate funds thereto.

We recommended, and the Municipal Government agreed that from this day forward, to provide the required signboards in conspicuous places within the agency premises and at the project site of all infrastructure projects and other major programs and activities, pursuant to the policy on full disclosure as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2013-004 and DILG MC 2010-101.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Engineer to:
Install water meters and repair damaged one to ensure accurate and correct billing and collection of water consumptions
Improve collections efficiency by imposing appropriate sanctions to delinquent consumers.

We recommended that: Management: Identify the Semi-expendable items/equipment and properties turned-over to the barangays and other entities to facilitate proper derecognition and transfer of accounts; Institute security measures to limit access of assets to authorized personnel only and maintain appropriate records as to whereabouts of equipment, vehicles and heavy equipment.

1.5.1 Required the  Municipal  Mayor  (MM)  to  direct  the  Municipal
          Accountant (MA) to secure copies of  turn-over  documents  of  the
          Evacuation Center and Various Supplies for ECCD,  to  support  the
          JEV recording of the Assets and Grants and Donations accounts.

2.7.1  We  recommended  the  MM  to  direct  the  following  concerned
          officials:


              1.  The  Municipal  Engineer  (ME)  to  submit   to   the   MA
                 Certificates of Project Completion/Turn-over to  facilitate
                 transfer of  completed  projects  to  the  appropriate  PPE
                 accounts.


               2. The MA to record the Cost of completed  projects  and  the
                  corresponding  accumulated  depreciation  in  the   books,
                  pursuant to IPSAS 17 and MNGAS.


                 Prepare Disbursement  Voucher  (DV)  to  return  unexpended
                 project funds to the source agency.

          2.7.1.3      The Municipal Treasurer (MT) to return the unexpended
                 balance of NGA and/or Other LGU funds to its source  agency
                 or Bureau of  Treasury,  as  the  case  may  be,  to  fully
                 liquidate the funds received.

3.4.1 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that:

              1. The Inventory Committee to assign a unique Property  Number
                 for each PPE item following the required code format in the
                 numbering system as provided in Section 5 of the Circular.


              2. The Inventory Committee to  prepare  a  Physical  Inventory
                 Plan (PIP) and submit the same to the  COA  Audit  Team  at
                 least ten calendar  days  before  the  scheduled  start  of
                 inventory  taking  activities  in   compliance   with   the
                 pertinent provisions of Section 5 of the Circular.


              3. Each office of the LGU to fill-up the Inventory Count  Form
                 and submit the same to the Property Unit for  verification.
                 The form will then be used by the  Inventory  Committee  as
                 basis in the preparation of its PIP and will be  considered
                 as part of their inventory working paper.


              4. Moreover, the Inventory Committee  to  record/document  the
                 physical count  daily  using  the  ICF  per  PPE  sub-major
                 account group and will be the basis in the  preparation  of
                 RPCPPE after the physical count.


              5. The Property Unit to  prepare  a  List  of  PPEs  Found  at
                 Station  (Annex  B  of  the  Circular)  and  List  of  Non-
                 Existing/Missing PPEs (Annex C  of  the  Circular)  during
                 physical inventory and submit these lists to the Accounting
                 Unit for recording in the books of accounts of the  LGU  to
                 establish the existence and proper  accountability  of  the
                 PPE accounts.


              6. The LCE to ensure that the guidelines and procedures in the
                 conduct of  physical  count  of  PPE  as  provided  in  COA
                 Circular 2020-006 are strictly observed to  facilitate  the
                 one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.


              7. Conduct proper disposal proceeding after receipt of the COA
                 Appraisal Review Report and  derecognized  from  the  books
                 disposed PPE, including missing PPEs in accordance  Section
                 8 of Circular.


              8. Identify the Semi-expendable items/equipment and properties
                 turned-over  to  the  barangays  and  other   entities   to
                 facilitate proper derecognition and transfer of accounts.

4.6.1 We recommended that the Mayor and HRMO prioritize the  filing-up
          of  the  MDRRM  Officer  and  staff  following  the  qualification
          standards, position titles and salary grades set by the IRR of  RA
          10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular  No.2014-1  as
          these are mandatory positions to provide adequate support  to  the
          various MDRRM activities.

5.10.1     To strengthen the  risk  management  capabilities  of  the
           Municipal Government and enable it to manage its vulnerabilities
           in the event of disruptive events, we recommended that the Local
           Chief Executive (LCE) institutionalize the PSCP  by  creating  a
           working group that will perform the following activities:


     5.10.1.1 to conduct risk assessment;
           5.10.1.2 to identify critical processes and functions;
           5.10.1.3  to  determine  scenarios  that  may   disrupt   normal
     operation;
           5.10.1.4 to conduct risk analysis and impact analysis;
           5.10.1.5    to formulate the PSCP based on the identified  risks
                  on critical processes and functions and its related  steps
                  to be followed to eliminate, if not mitigate,  the  impact
                  of the determined  disruptions  following  the  prescribed
                  template and be guided by the PSCP guidebook; and
           5.10.1.6    to submit plan to the MDRRMO for review, to the  LCE
                  for approval, and to the Sangguniang Bayan for the passage
                  of a resolution to institutionalize the PSCP.


       5.10.2 We recommended also that Management improve  their  monitoring
            of operational requirements like the submission of PSCP.

1. We recommended that the LCE direct the  Municipal  Planning  and
           Development Officer to establish  GAD  database  containing  sex
           disaggregated data to serve as source  of  information  for  the
           conduct of gender analysis, identify gender issues and determine
           gender responsive PPAs and incorporate them in the GAD Plan  and
           Budget for costing.

7.5    We  recommended  that  the  management  create  and   establish
         Monitoring and Evaluation Team in  consonance  with  the  provision
         Section 5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to evaluate  the
         LGU GAD policies, programs and projects.

8.4.1.1     The Municipal Budget  Officer  to  be  accurate  and
                  consistent in the posting or recording of budgeted  amount
                  in his report and inform the Municipal Accountant  of  any
                  changes in the amount reflected  in  the  records  of  the
                  Municipal Budget Office so that any changes  made  in  the
                  budgeted amount will be presented in the SCBAA  under  the
                  budgeted amount column; and

           8.4.1.2     Based on the information  obtain  by  the  Municipal
                  Accountant from the Municipal Budget Officer, prepare  and
                  include  the  required  reconciliation  on  the  Notes  to
                  Financial Statements.

9.4.1  We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  prepare  the
          required disclosure on the Reconciliation of Net Cash  Flows  from
          Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit) in NFS  as  provided  in
          IPSAS 2 and Annex G of COA Circular No. 2016-004.

10.5.1    Required the Municipal Mayor, MMO  Staff,  MHO  and  other
             concerned officials and employees to account for the remaining
             quantities comprising the difference of P1.541  million,  duly
             supported with proofs of receipt by the beneficiaries;

          2. Submit/prepare Work and Financial Plan, embodying among others
             the following:

              1.  Criteria/basis  for  entitlement  of  the  welfare  goods
                 assistance;
              2. Mode of distribution and proof of receipt required/needed;
              3.  Implementing  Office  and  personnel   involve   in   the
                 distribution; and
                   4. Other necessary  guidelines/policies  to  ensure  the
                      smooth and orderly distribution of goods.

A.4.1    Required the Municipal Mayor, MMO Staff, MHO,  MNAO  other
             concerned officials and  employees  to  submit  the  following
             documents relevant to the Food Supplies Expenses for the Audit
             Team’s examination and validation:

             A.4.1.1    Work  and  Financial  Plan  of  each  program   and
                    activities,  wherein  food  supplies  are  procured  and
                    distributed
             A.4.1.2  Complete Attendance Sheet of each activity
             A.4.1.3  Proof of actual  distribution  of  goods  (Matrix  of
                    Distribution,      duly      received       by       the
                    beneficiaries/participants.
             A.4.1.4  Pictures
             A.4.1.5  Activity/Program Terminal  Report  or  Accomplishment
        Report


B.5.1 We required the Municipal Mayor to

              B.5.1.2  Direct the MMO Staff preparing the Summary  of  Fuel
                    Consumptions  to  submit  the  following  documents   to
                    support the utilization and usages of FOL products  such
                    as:

                      B.5.1.2.1   Fuel  Consumption  Report-indicating   the
        purposes
                     B.5.1.2.2 Driver’s Trip Ticket-indicating the purposes,
                            destination and name & signature passengers
                    B.5.1.2.3       Program   of    Works,    Accomplishment
                            Report/Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA) and
                            pictures  (for  rehabilitation  and  repair  of
                            roads)


         B.5.2    Submit satisfactory explanation or  justification  on  the
             inconsistencies between the fuel consumption report  and  trip
             tickets as against the logbook of the FOL custodian.

1. We recommended the Municipal Mayor to  require  the  Municipal
             Engineer

             10.4.1.1 to call the attention of the Contractor to facilitate
                    completion of the project
             10.4.1.2  determine the Negative Slippage incurred and require
                    the contractor to submit a Catch-up Plan
                 3. Submit Work Suspension Order (s) and Resumption  Orders;
                    and
             10.4.1.4  Submit  Revised   Contract   Duration   and   Target
             Completion

13.4.1 We recommended the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Engineer to:

             13.4.1.1  Submit proof that the  case  was  already  filed  to
                     appropriate Court and to apprise the Audit Team of the
                     Status thereof.

             11.4.1.2 Recommend Black-listing of the defaulting  Contractor
                     to PCAB, to avoid participating  in  other  government
                     procurement projects.

14.5.1 We recommended the  Municipal  Mayor  to  institute  security
measures to


              14.5.1.1 limit access of assets to authorized personnel only
              14.5.1.2 assigned personnel to maintain  appropriate  records
                    as to  whereabouts  of  equipment,  vehicles  and  heavy
                    equipment.

15.5.1     We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the
             Municipal Engineer to notify and require the  Contractor/s  to
             rectify defects  noted  by  the  COA  TAS  on  the  three  (3)
             infrastructure  projects   to   avoid   further   damage   and
             deterioration thereof.

We recommended that: The Municipal Treasurer cancels the unclaimed stale checks and furnishes the Accountant with the original Disbursement Vouchers (DV) and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher following the procedures prescribed in Section 59 of Chapter 3 of the MNGAS for LGUs Volume I, to ensure recording of such cancellation; The Municipal Accountant, if possible, coordinates with the bank to determine whether checks claimed by the payees were not yet presented for payment and secure the addressees of the payees for notification of the return of stale checks in their possession for replacement; and The Municipal Accountant exerts effort to review the long outstanding/stale checks and for those without any check number, disbursement voucher number and date of issue, the Municipal Accountant should get the necessary information and if per record these have been stale for many years, consider the reversion of these payables to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: Make the necessary adjusting entries in the book of accounts of the LGU to reflect the correct Cash in Bank balance as shown in the Adjusted Cash in Bank balance per Bank Reconciliation and to correctly state the balances of the affected accounts whether real or nominal as of December 31, 2020; and Ensure that all valid reconciling items to be recorded and adjusted in the books are properly supported with complete documents.

We recommended that the Accounting Office: Prepare a reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts of the respective funds; and Instruct the Accounting personnel assigned to journalize the financial transactions to record and set-up simultaneously the reciprocal account in the different funds affected and for the Municipal Accountant to constantly review the recording of such transactions.

We recommended the Management to: Post its procurement through emergency cases in the GPPB Online Portal; Ensure that Omnibus Sworn Statement (OSS) contains the name of the procurement undertaken and must be an original copy; Make certain that suppliers were capable to bid for the procurement opportunity based on their computed NFCC. Also, cause the submission of Audited Financial Statements (AFS) of other winning suppliers as supporting document to disbursement vouchers; Observe the procurement procedures for Negotiated Procurement through Emergency cases under Section 53.2, RIRR of RA NO. 9184 and ensure that the RFQ provides for the prescribed period for submission of documentary requirements by the suppliers; and In all future procurement projects, the Requisitioning Officer and Technical Working Group (TWG) will ensure Purchase Request (PR) and Purchase Orders (PO) shall be carefully scrutinize so that all needed specifications of the requisitioned items are included therein that would be useful in determining the price reasonableness and compliance with specifications.

We recommended that the LGU adopt a policy requiring the business owner to submit their income tax return to support the sworn statement of sales declaration in order to ensure that the declared sales are more or less reliable as a basis for assessment of business tax. Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer to utilize the Alpha List of Income Tax Payor prepared by the Municipal Accountant as reference in making business tax assessment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer (ME) to exert more efforts to facilitate timely completion of the projects to ensure that the 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer: Prepare POW based on the Standard Pay Items; Provide adequate detail on quantities such as length, area and volume and Cost Estimates; Complete set of Approved Plans and Drawings; Conduct a time and motion study of each heavy equipment in order to come-up with a standard fuel consumption per km/day/hour that will serve as basis in POW preparation of future projects; and Require the testing of all materials subject to test used in the project from a duly accredited DPWH testing laboratory.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that: The Inventory Committee to assign a unique Property Number for each PPE item following the required code format in the numbering system as provided in Section 5 of the Circular. The Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and submit the same to the COA Audit Team at least ten calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities in compliance with the pertinent provisions of Section 5 of the Circular. Each office of the LGU to fill-up the Inventory Count Form and submit the same to the Property Unit for verification. The form will then be used by the Inventory Committee as basis in the preparation of its PIP and will be considered as part of their inventory working paper. Moreover, the Inventory Committee to record/document the physical count daily using the ICF per PPE sub-major account group and will be the basis in the preparation of RPCPPE after the physical count. The Property Unit to prepare a List of PPEs Found at Station (Annex B of the Circular) and List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs (Annex C of the Circular) during physical inventory and submit these lists to the Accounting Unit for recording in the books of accounts of the LGU to establish the existence and proper accountability of the PPE accounts. The LCE to ensure that the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE as provided in COA Circular 2020-006 are strictly observed to facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances. Conduct proper disposal proceeding after receipt of the COA Appraisal Review Report and derecognized from the books disposed PPE, including missing PPEs in accordance Section 8 of Circular. Identify the Semi-expendable items/equipment and properties turned-over to the barangays and other entities to facilitate proper derecognition and transfer of accounts.

We required the Municipal Mayor to: Direct the MMO Staff preparing the Summary of Fuel Consumptions to submit the following documents to support the utilization and usages of FOL products such as: Fuel Consumption Report-indicating the purposes; Driver’s Trip Ticket-indicating the purposes, destination and name & signature passengers; Program of Works, Accomplishment Report/Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA) and pictures (for rehabilitation and repair of roads). Submit satisfactory explanation or justification on the inconsistencies between the fuel consumption report and trip tickets as against the logbook of the FOL custodian.
Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer consider the closure of LBP-Dipolog branch account and transfer it to LBP-Sindangan branch since it is more accessible and convenient for the LGU.

Recommendation
1.5 We recommended that:
1.5.1 The Municipal Treasurer cancels the unclaimed stale checks and furnishes
the Accountant with the original Disbursement Vouchers (DV) and its
supporting documents, for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher
following the procedures prescribed in Section 59 of Chapter 3 of the
MNGAS for LGUs Volume I, to ensure recording of such cancellation;
1.5.2 The Municipal Accountant, if possible, coordinates with the bank to
determine whether checks claimed by the payees were not yet presented
for payment and secure the addressees of the payees for notification of the
return of stale checks in their possession for replacement; and
1.5.3 The Municipal Accountant exerts effort to review the long outstanding/stale
checks and for those without any check number, disbursement voucher
number and date of issue, the Municipal Accountant should get the
necessary information and if per record these have been stale for many
years, consider the reversion of these payables to the unappropriated
surplus of the General Fund.

Recommendation
2.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
2.7.1 Make the necessary adjusting entries in the book of accounts of the LGU
to reflect the correct Cash in Bank balance as shown in the Adjusted Cash
in Bank balance per Bank Reconciliation and to correctly state the
balances of the affected accounts whether real or nominal as of
December 31, 2020; and
2.7.2 Ensure that all valid reconciling items to be recorded and adjusted in the
books are properly supported with complete documents;
2.8 Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer consider the closure of
LBP-Dipolog branch account and transfer it to LBP-Sindangan branch since it
is more accessible and convenient for the LGU.

Recommendation
3.5 We recommended that the Management:
3.5.1 Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to observe the
procedures outlined in Section 7 of the Circular in order to exhaust available
means to locate missing PPEs;
3.5.2 Conduct proper disposal proceeding after receipt of the COA Appraisal
Review Report and derecognized from the books disposed PPE, including
missing PPEs in accordance Section 8 of Circular;
3.5.3 Identify the Semi-expendable items/equipment and properties turned-over
to the barangays and other entities to facilitate proper derecognition and
transfer of accounts;
3.5.4 Provide complete information of PPEs indicated on the RCPPE including
property number, status, location and person/s accountable; and
3.5.5 Institute security measures to limit access of assets to authorized personnel
only and maintain appropriate records as to whereabouts of equipment,
vehicles and heavy equipment.

Recommendation
4.7 We recommended that the Accounting Office:
4.7.1 Prepare a reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts and take up the
necessary adjustment in the books of accounts of the respective funds; and
4.7.2 Instruct the Accounting personnel assigned to journalize the financial
transactions to record and set-up simultaneously the reciprocal account in
the different funds affected and for the Municipal Accountant to constantly
review the recording of such transactions.

Recommendations
5.5 We recommended the Management to:
5.5.1 Post its procurement through emergency cases in the GPPB Online Portal.
5.5.2 Ensure that Omnibus Sworn Statement (OSS) contains the name of the
procurement undertaken and must be an original copy.
5.5.3 Make certain that suppliers were capable to bid for the procurement
opportunity based on their computed NFCC. Also, cause the submission of
Audited Financial Statements (AFS) of other winning suppliers as
supporting document to disbursement vouchers.
5.5.4 Observe the procurement procedures for Negotiated Procurement through
Emergency cases under Section 53.2, RIRR of RA NO. 9184 and ensure
that the RFQ provides for the prescribed period for submission of
documentary requirements by the suppliers.
5.5.5 In all future procurement projects, the Requisitioning Officer and Technical
Working Group (TWG) will ensure Purchase Request (PR) and Purchase
Orders (PO) shall be carefully scrutinize so that all needed specifications of
the requisitioned items are included therein that would be useful in
determining the price reasonableness and compliance with specifications.

Recommendation
7.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer (ME) to
exert more efforts to facilitate timely completion of the projects to ensure that the
20% of the IRA is optimally utilized to help achieve desirable socio-economic
development and environmental outcomes.

Recommendation
8.7 We recommended that the Municipal Engineer:
8.7.1 Prepare POW based on the Standard Pay Items;
8.7.2 Provide adequate detail on quantities such as length, area and volume and
Cost Estimates;
8.7.3 Complete set of Approved Plans and Drawings;
46
8.7.4 Conduct a time and motion study of each heavy equipment in order to
come-up with a standard fuel consumption per km/day/hour that will serve
as basis in POW preparation of future projects; and
8.7.5 Require the testing of all materials subject to test used in the project from a
duly accredited DPWH testing laboratory.

Recommendation
9.5 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to ensure
that DVs are supported with correct and valid documents (SDs) to established
responsibility and accountability. As such Fuel Consumption Report should
reconcile with the POWs and Logbook; receipt, custody and issuances of
materials are recorded properly accounted for.

Require the Municipal Mayor, Accountant and Treasurer to inform the Contractor, Winley Y. Tan the overpayment and collect therefrom the amount of P660,003.00 as soon as possible, otherwise Notice of Disallowance (ND) will be issued thereon.

The Municipal Mayor to direct the following concerned officials:
A. The Municipal Engineer to:
• Constantly monitor project implementation to expedite project completion and ensure compliance with government standards.
• Submit to the Accountant needed documents of completed projects to support reclassification of CIP into PPE.
• Coordinate with the DILG/DA/DAR and other source agencies to validate the contractor’s accomplishment to expedite the remaining funds will be released as soon as possible.
B. The Municipal Accountant to:
• Regularly prepare and submit SORD and liquidation documents to the source agencies to ensure prompt release of subsequent tranches and reconcile both accounting records.
• Record the Cost of completed projects and the corresponding accumulated depreciation in the books, pursuant to PPSAS.
C. The Municipal Treasurer to:
• Return the unexpended balance of BUB and other NGA funds to the source agency or Bureau of Treasury, as the case may be to fully liquidate the funds received.

A. Prescribed Water Rates as embodied in the Local Revenue Code were not systematically carried out and
B. Sanctions were not imposed to Delinquent Customers thus, affecting the collection efficiency of the water works.
C. The management did not apply accrual basis in accounting Service Income earned from waterworks system fees resulting to understatement of the account by P0.10 Million.
The Municipal Treasurer:
• Impose the prescribed Water Rates as embodied in the Local Revenue Code to enable the LGU earn substantial amount of income that could be useful for betterment of the water works services.
• Improved collections efficiency by imposing appropriate sanctions to delinquent customers
• Submit the monthly report of bills rendered to market occupant which will be made as basis in the accrual and recording of rental income.
B. The Municipal Engineer
• Look into ways and means of improving the services so that all customers could enjoy the same privilege of having sufficient supply of water at all times.
• Replace damaged and defective water meters to have an accurate water consumption and billing.
• Further, investigate causes extremely high water consumption before granting discounts to customers and let them repair leaks of pipelines immediately to avoid further loss of water and revenue.
C. Municipal Accountant
• Apply accrual basis as required under Par. 7, PPSAS 1 in recording rental income based on the monthly report submitted by the Municipal Treasurer.

Require the Municipal Assessor to follow the process of tax assessment by following the assessment calendar as required in the Mass Appraisal Guidebook in order to implement general revision in a manner in consonance to the provision of Section 215 of RA 7160; Revised the Tax Declaration under TD No. 04-0001-00257; 04-0001-00838; 04-0001-285; 04-0001-285 and 04-0017-00200 effecting the changes as observed in audit and submit to the Auditor a certified copy of the revised TDs.; and Submit action plan to conduct a field inspection and verification in order to check whether there are other properties which might be misclassified, not declared or erroneously declared.

The management to immediately correct the noted deficiencies and comply as well with the Notice to Correct Violations of the Bureau of Fire to ensure the safety of the occupants of the Buildings owned and so that no fines, penalties, abatements or closures are imposed on the agency.

1. Municipal Accountant
• to deduct equivalent TA of Officials with assigned/using government vehicles.
• Require drivers to attached trip tickets and accomplishments as supporting documents to their salaries.
2. Vice Mayor and Sanguniang Bayan Members
• to maintain Logbook for each vehicle to record the whereabouts of the vehicle.

1. Municipal Treasurer
• Re-issue ARE/PAR of the vehicle. Motor vehicles to the actual user of the property and vehicles to ensure full responsibility and accountability over the assets
• Renew or update existing ARE/PAR of all PPE that are more than three (3) and are transferred to another user.
• Require the Official and employees issued with government vehicles to maintain logbook of all vehicles assigned at their Offices.
2. Vice Mayor and Sanguniang Bayan Members
1. Acknowledge the Receipt of the Vehicles issued to the Sangguniang Bayan; and
2. Maintain Logbook for each vehicle to record the whereabouts of the vehicle.

We required the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Agriculturist, PESO and Sangguniang Bayan Members to:
• Make a comprehensive study on how to make the facilities operational and make it viable and responsive to the needs of the constituents.
• Enact an Ordinance regulating the Fees rate of the facilities
• Provide adequate and trained staff/personnel to man the operations of the facilities, and
• Appropriate funds for improvement, repair and maintenance of the facility to maintain quality service to customers and general public.

3.8 We recommendedthat management thru the Local Disaster Risk Reduction
Management Office to:

preparedness as specified in the Minimum Standards for Disaster
Preparedness, especially on the items identified;
b. Include in the LDRRM Investment Plan, the establishment of toilets for PWDs
as well as an appropriate storage area in their designated evacuation center,
and ensure its implementation;
c. Install Rainwater Collection System as alternative sources of water supply for
the evacuation center; and
d. Evaluate and monitor the existing equipment and facilities for disaster
preparedness to ensure compliance with the minimum standards and to
further improve the LGUs capabilities to be disaster resilient.

Municipal Accountant
1.Prepare the Journal Voucher to record the adjusted beginning balance (depreciated replacement cost) of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card as well as the adjustments due to the errors in the computation of depreciation and provision of salvage value (Annex D of the Circular), respectively;
2..Support the JV with working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry;
3.Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
4. Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
Municipal Treasurer
1. 5. Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
2. 6. Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. (Annex E of the Circular)
Municipal Engineer
