The Municipal Agriculturist facilitate the submission of inventory report every reporting date for the Municipal Accountant to properly booked up the accurate amount of biological assets owned by the Municipality so that biological . assets which the LGU have no more claims or already non- existent could be dropped from the books of accounts .
The Municipal Accountant facilitate the adjustment of the Plants and Trees and Other Bearer Biological Asset accounts due to erroneous recording.

to the Municipal Treasurer take the necessary measures to update the Report on Real Property Assessment and the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/Taxpayer’s Index Card, respectively, in order that the amounts due and collectible for the years since the receivables were not booked up could easily be determined.
Based on the RPTAR/Taxpayers Index Card, the Municipal Accountant should establish the RPT/SET Receivables then set up the receivables as estimated. Upon collection of Real Property Tax from taxpayers, these receivable accounts should be adjusted.
The same procedure should be done for the subsequent years.

The Requisitioning Officer and Technical Working Group (TWG) will ensure Purchase Requisitioned Items and Proposed Terms for the Procurement specifically, related points... (table continues)
1. Management to l abide with the limitations in NBC 446-A and other government rules and regulations on the purchase of motor vehicles to avoid acquisition of old vehicles that are prone to damage and repairs. Thus, procurement of brand new units are encourage to minimize incurrence of repairs and maintenance cost.
2. Require the supplier to make necessary repair or replacement of the air-conditioning unit of Chevrolet Colorado which is not functioning effectively.
3. Finally, submit a satisfactory explanation and/or justification why the Chevrolet Colorado was awarded to the supplier despite of the non- compliance with the specifications in the PO on engine displacement and transmission.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following department heads for the compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015:
Municipal Accountant
Prepare the Journal Voucher to record the adjusted beginning balance (depreciated replacement cost) of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card as well as the adjustments due to the errors in the computation of depreciation and provision of salvage value  (Annex D of the Circular), respectively;
Support the JV with working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry;
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
Municipal General Services Officer
Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. (Annex E of the Circular)
Municipal Engineer
Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
Management Comment:
Management commented that they are still on the process of reconciling the actual inventory and accounting records and agreed with the recommendations stated.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist facilitate the submission of inventory report every reporting date in order for the Municipal Accountant to properly booked up the accurate amount of biological assets owned by the Municipality so that biological assets which the LGU have no more claims or already non-existent could be dropped from the books of accounts . We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant facilitate the adjustment of the Plants and Trees and Other Bearer Biological Asset accounts due to erroneous recording.
Management Comment:
Management commented that the Municipal Accountant together with Municipal Agriculturist will reconcile the balances, gather the needed documents such as proof of receipts for those assets for distribution and will prepare the adjusting entries accordingly to affect the correct balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer take the necessary measures to update the Report on Real Property Assessment and the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/Taxpayer’s Index Card, respectively, in order that the amounts due and collectible for the years since the receivables were not booked up  could easily be determined.
Based on the RPTAR/Taxpayers Index Card, the Municipal Accountant should establish the RPT/SET Receivables then set up the receivables as estimated. Upon collection of Real Property Tax from taxpayers, these receivable accounts should be adjusted. The same procedure should be done for the subsequent years.
Management Comment:
Management commented that the Municipal Assessor will provide the corresponding schedules and the Municipal Accountant will set up the receivables accordingly and the end or at the beginning of the CY.

We required the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Agriculturist, PESO and Sangguniang Bayan Members to:
Make a comprehensive study on how to make the facilities operational and make it viable and responsive to the needs of the constituents.
Enact an Ordinance regulating the Fees rate of the facilities
Provide adequate and trained staff/personnel to man the operations of the facilities, and
Appropriate funds for improvement, repair and maintenance of the facility to maintain quality service to customers and general public
Management Comments:
Management commented the following:
On the Fish Landing: The Municipal Officials are still on the process of finalizing an ordinance to make the facilities operational and make it viable and responsive to the needs of the constituents.
On the Sardines Processing: They will conduct study and proposals to address the issues faced by the women’s association in order for the facility to become operative.
On the Pancit Making and Sewing: They will conduct further study to equipped the workers the necessary skills for the processes. They admitted that they procure first the equipments without training the workers first which resulted to the products becoming unfit for commercial use.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Mayor to call the attention of the contractors of the three (3) projects that remained not completed to facilitate completion thereof as soon as possible;
For the 17 projects that are physically completed, to prepare the necessary reports as soon as possible  and submit to the funding agency and request for inspection to this Office, together with its supporting documents to avoid delay in project inspection and evaluation by the Audit Team and COA-RTS; and
The Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to monitor the contractors’ compliance with the contract period/ duration and take appropriate legal action against the contractor for delayed completion of the project including imposition of Liquidated Damages.
Management Comment:
Management agreed and promised to comply with the recommendations stated.

In view of the foregoing observations we recommended that:
In all future procurement projects, the Requisitioning Officer and Technical Working Group (TWG) will ensure Purchase Request (PR) shall be carefully scrutinize so that all needed specifications of the equipment are included therein in order to give equal opportunity to prospective bidders to be able to offer an informed bids that would be useful in determining the price reasonableness and compliance with specifications.
Management shall abide with the limitations in NBC 446-A and other government rules and regulations on the purchase of motor vehicles to avoid acquisition of old vehicles that are prone to damage and repairs. Thus, procurement of brand new units are encourage to minimize incurrence of repairs and maintenance cost.
Require the supplier to make necessary repair or replacement of the air-conditioning unit of Chevrolet Colorado which is not functioning effectively.
Finally, submit a satisfactory explanation and/or justification why the Chevrolet Colorado was awarded to the supplier despite of the non-compliance with the specifications in the PO on engine displacement and transmission.
Management Comment:
Management has agreed to comply with the recommendations and promised to submit the justification on the procurement that transpired.

We required the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Engineer to
impose the prescribe rates as embodied in the Local Revenue Code to enable the LGU earn substantial amount of income that could be useful for betterment of the water works services.
Improve collections efficiency by imposing appropriate sanctions to delinquent customers.
look into ways and means of improving the services so that all customers could enjoy the same privilege of having sufficient supply of water at all times.
Further, investigate causes extremely high water consumption before granting discounts to customers and let them repair leaks of pipelines immediately to avoid further loss of water and revenue.
Management Comment:
Management has already investigated about the issues brought out by the Audit Team assures that all recommendations were properly noted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through the members of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) adopt the following courses of action:
Stop the operation of open dumpsite;
Procurement of recycling equipment to fully equip the MRF;
Strictly enforce the waste segregation policy at source
Comply with the applicable requirements for the operation of closed dumpsite as provided in DENR Administrative Order No. 9.
Management Comments:
Management commented that the rehabilitation and safe closure of dumpsite is now on-going with its construction of perimeter fence, one (1) unit Residual Containment Area (RCA) with the main Material Recovery Facility (MRF) and promised to strictly observed and implement the stated recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit the required reports for the Municipal Accountant to record completed and accepted BUB projects to the appropriate asset accounts to ensure infrastructure asset and government equity accounts are correctly reported as of year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer remit immediately the unutilized balance of BuB funds and other NGA funded project to the Source Agency or Bureau of Treasury in compliance with the regulations.

The Municipal Engineer shall closely monitor the implementation of ADM project to ensure the completion thereof within the Project Implementation Schedule to avoid sanctions and promptly deliver the project to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that management:

1. Immediately execute lease contracts embodying the terms and conditions of the lease to be observed by the contracting parties, the rate of rent including additional fees for better located stalls, rental fee for extensions constructed, if any and penalties for failure to pay within the prescribed period, in accordance with the provisions of the Revised Revenue Code; and 	
2. Enforce surcharges on late payment to deter tenants/lessees neglecting their obligation.

We recommended that management promote the establishment of Material Recovery Facility to all barangays, set-up a scheduled collection date of accumulated solid waste at least once a month and exert additional effort to fully implement the programs, projects and activities embodied in the MSWMP to realize the Municipality’s goals on solid waste management towards public health and environmental.

We recommended that projects and activities to be charged to unexpended balances of LDDRMF of previous years be included in the LDRRMFIP under a separate caption for the comprehensive formulation and implementation of disaster risk reduction and management activities. We also recommended that separate registries for QRF, MOOE and CO be maintained for easy monitoring of disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and other personnel concerned to deduct only the mandatory contributions for PAG-IBIG and other institutions as directed in the above-mentioned memorandum circular and include all income and benefits paid by the LGU to its officers and employees in the computation of gross income to correctly impose and withhold income taxes and subsequent remittance thereof to the BIR to ensure compliance with tax laws and avoid penalties, surcharges, and imprisonment, in worst cases. Hence, recomputation of compensation income tax for CY 2017 be made and record the overpayment to officials and employees due to understated income tax by debiting Due from Officers and Employees and crediting Due to BIR.

We recommended that management effect the transfer of its public infrastructure accounts to the books of accounts of the LGU pursuant to the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards and COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following department heads for the compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015:

Municipal Accountant
Prepare the Journal Voucher to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card (Annex D of the Circular), respectively;
Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry;
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

Municipal General Services Officer

Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. (Annex E of the Circular)

Municipal Engineer

Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned LGU offices and associations to submit to COA the documentary requirements mentioned in Finding No. 1 and 2 and to refrain from disbursing funds without complete documentation in future related transactions. Likewise, it is recommended that the Inspection and Property Officers submit written comments/explanations on the observations noted in Finding No. 3.

	Further, we recommended that the focal person and association presidents, thru the Office of the Municipal Mayor, coordinate with their partner agencies for the conduct of training to avoid further delay in the implementation of projects.

	Lastly, the LGU through the Municipal Mayor, require the LPRAT to meet on a quarterly basis and discuss the progress of BUB and other partner agencies’ projects and submit status or progress report to the Local Chief Executive for proper implementation and monitoring of projects.

observations.reason:
HGDG were still not administered to projects by attribution.
GAD Database were yet to be established by the LGU.

Submission of financial reports/records/journals and supporting documents to COA was delayed. The Municipal Treasurer justified that due to the distance of the AGDB from their LGU, a few of the collectors on field and other pressing concerns of her office and that of the LGU, turnover of collections and preparation of RCDs were done 3 times a week instead of on a day-to-day basis. Nevertheless, the Municipal Treasurer assured that all collections for the week were turned over to her by the collectors and deposited before weekend.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit the required reports for the Municipal Accountant to record completed and accepted BUB projects to the appropriate asset accounts to ensure infrastructure asset and government equity accounts are correctly reported as of year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer remit immediately the unutilized balance of BuB funds and other NGA funded project to the Source Agency or Bureau of Treasury in compliance with the regulations.

The Municipal Engineer, MPDC and BAC Secretariat are enjoined to strictly comply Section 3.1 of COA Circular No.2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 by submitting the required project documents to expedite audit actions.

The Municipal Engineer shall closely monitor the implementation of ADM project to ensure the completion thereof within the Project Implementation Schedule to avoid sanctions and promptly deliver the project to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that management:
1.	Immediately execute lease contracts embodying the terms and conditions of the lease to be observed by the contracting parties, the rate of rent including additional fees for better located stalls, rental fee for extensions constructed, if any and penalties for failure to pay within the prescribed period, in accordance with the provisions of the Revised Revenue Code; and
2.	Enforce surcharges on late payment to deter tenants/lessees neglecting their obligation.

We recommended that management promote the establishment of Material Recovery Facility to all barangays, set-up a scheduled collection date of accumulated solid waste at least once a month and exert additional effort to fully implement the programs, projects and activities embodied in the MSWMP to realize the Municipality’s goals on solid waste management towards public health and environmental.

We recommended that projects and activities to be charged to unexpended balances of LDDRMF of previous years be included in the LDRRMFIP under a separate caption for the comprehensive formulation and implementation of disaster risk reduction and management activities. We also recommended that separate registries for QRF, MOOE and CO be maintained for easy monitoring of disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and other personnel concerned to deduct only the mandatory contributions for PAG-IBIG and other institutions as directed in the above-mentioned memorandum circular and include all income and benefits paid by the LGU to its officers and employees in the computation of gross income to correctly impose and withhold income taxes and subsequent remittance thereof to the BIR to ensure compliance with tax laws and avoid penalties, surcharges, and imprisonment, in worst cases. Hence, recomputation of compensation income tax for CY 2017 be made and record the overpayment to officials and employees due to understated income tax by debiting Due from Officers and Employees and crediting Due to BIR.

observations.reason: The transfer of public infrastructure accounts to the book of accounts of the LGU were not entirely in accordance with the PPSAS and COA Circular No. 2016-004.

Some of the officials were still issued more than one phone.
The LGU did not issue Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment.

Officials and employees were yet to return the livestock received.
During the cash examination of some AOs on November 4, 2016, the Audit Team still noted delays in the remittances and reporting of collections.

Based on the subsidiary ledger submitted, the Municipal Accountant is yet to establish SL specifying the 40-40-20 allocation.
Provide subsidiary ledger specifying the 40-40-20 allocation of the Philhealth Capitation Fund for easier monitoring of the fund disposition.
The LGU were yet to procure recycling equipments.
During the cash examination of some AOs on November 4, 2016, the Audit Team still noted delays in the remittances and reporting of collections.

The Municipal Treasurer justified that due to the distance of the AGDB from their LGU, a few of the collectors on field and other pressing concerns of her office and that of the LGU, turnover of collections and preparation of RCDs were done 3 times a week instead of on a day-to-day basis. Nevertheless, the Municipal Treasurer assured that all collections for the week were turned over to her by the collectors and deposited before weekend.

1. The remittance and reporting of collections by the accountable officers were delayed by 3 to 20 days contrary to Section 29 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System.
2. Adjustments are required for the (over) under recording of collections in order to provide accurate entries in the cashbooks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the Work and Financial Plan containing therein the specific programs/projects and initiate the request of approval from the Sangguniang Bayan through a resolution ratifying the incurrence of such expenditures.

We recommended that the Local School Boards prepare a budget for the utilization of the Special Education Fund in responsive to the priority areas emphasized in Sections 100 and 272 of the Local Government Code of 1991 (RA 7160) and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars Nos. 01 s. 1998, 01-A dated March 14, 2000 and 01-B dated June 25, 2001 and refrain from expending the same with programs, projects and activities not directly benefit the students.
We recommended that the LGU effect the transfer of the unexpended LDRRMF and comply with the deficiencies noted above.
observations.reason: The LGU transfers its unexpended appropriations every January of the ensuing year during the time when the accounting prepares the books of accounts for the month of December of the previous year to ensure that completeness of transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through the members of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) adopt the following courses of action: Stop the operation of open dumpsite; Procurement of recycling equipment to fully equip the MRF; Strictly enforce the waste segregation policy at source; and Establish a long-term storage facility for the residual wastes.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant expedite the preparation and timely submission of the required monthly financial reports and vouchers.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management effect the transfer of its public infrastructure accounts to the books of accounts of the LGU pursuant to the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards and COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following department heads for the compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015:
Municipal Accountant
Prepare the Journal Voucher to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card (Annex D of the Circular), respectively;
Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry;
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
Municipal General Services Officer
Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. (Annex E of the Circular)
Municipal Engineer
Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the Work and Financial Plan containing therein the specific programs/projects for the 20% Economic Development Fund.
We recommended further that prior authority from the local sanggunian be secured before disbursements of funds covered by lump-sum appropriations be made. Likewise, discontinue the practice of appropriating funds in lump-sum amounts and resort to line budgeting to ensure regularity and transparency in government transactions.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from charging wages of job order personnel whose duties and responsibilities and the nature of work done were not related to and/or connected with the different projects under the 20% EDF.
We recommended further that the Municipal Accountant meticulously check the appropriateness of entries made in the books of accounts for disbursements, whether or not the transactions are to be capitalized as part of the cost of the project or be expensed outright and be part of the regular MOOE of the LGU.

Recommendation:
It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Analyze transactions to ensure proper recording and classifying of accounts;
Effect necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the transactions in their  appropriate/correct accounts as discussed above; and
Ensure that the preparation of the Financial Statements of the LGU comply with the requirements of PPSAS 1, COA Resolution No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 and P.D. 1445.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned LGU offices and associations to submit to COA the documentary requirements mentioned in Finding No. 1 and 2 and to refrain from disbursing funds without complete documentation in future related transactions. Likewise, it is recommended that the Inspection and Property Officers submit written comments/explanations on the observations noted in Finding No. 3.
Further, we recommended that the focal person and association presidents, thru the Office of the Municipal Mayor, coordinate with their partner agencies for the conduct of training to avoid further delay in the implementation of projects.
Lastly, the LGU through the Municipal Mayor, require the LPRAT to meet on a quarterly basis and discuss the progress of BUB and other partner agencies’ projects and submit status or progress report to the Local Chief Executive for proper implementation and monitoring of projects.

Recommendation:
It is recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to analyze transactions to ensure proper recording and classifying of accounts as well as effect necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the transactions in their  appropriate/correct accounts as discussed above; and
Direct the Municipal Engineer to submit to COA, the lacking documentary requirements.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer:
Should procure medicines and supplies on a quarterly basis based on the rate of consumption of the preceding quarter through public bidding to avail of the best prices in the market.
Refrain from charging office supplies expense to the Philhealth Capitation Fund since these are not allowed in the Philhealth Circular and consequently, the LGU should replenish the amount utilized for the said purchases back to the Philhealth Capitation Fund.
Give preferential attention to the 40% fund intended for the purchases of drugs and medicine and properly monitor its disposition to avoid charges outside the scope of the fund.
Refrain from granting honoraria to officials and employees not directly involved in the operations of the health office as this constitutes irregular disbursement subject to disallowances.
Provide subsidiary ledger specifying the 40-40-20 allocation of the Philhealth Capitation Fund for easier monitoring of the fund disposition.
Reconcile the balances reflected in the books of accounts and the SORDs for proper recording and reporting.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the LGU stop the practice of remitting premium contributions of employer’s share based on 2% of the employee’s monthly basic salary.  Instead, strictly follow the maximum monthly compensation of Five thousand pesos (P5,000.00) in the computation of employer’s share as provided under Section 7 of RA No. 9679, otherwise known as the HDMF Fund law of 2009, unless amended.

Recommendation:
The end user with the assistance of the Technical Working Group must see to it that the ABC is anchored on cost-efficient and realistic amount.
The BAC should maximize the use of PhilGEPS other than the posting feature and always ensure the widest dissemination of the procurement opportunity to secure the price most advantageous to the procuring entity.
Though no longer required, the LGU may continue its old practice under the Local Government Code (Section 363) of posting the call for bids (now the IAEB) in at least three (3) publicly accessible and conspicuous places and sending by mail or otherwise to prospective bidders in the locality and nearby areas. This will ensure the widest publicity of the procurement activity and encourage more participants in the bidding process.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the BAC should be strict in requiring all suppliers to submit their current and updated Certificate of PhilGEPS Registration to conform to the requirements of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) refrain from providing for non-priority expenditures in the SEF Budget. Instead, allocate or use such fund for the priority programs/projects/activities as identified DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, 01-A and 01-B. Also, the LSB should evaluate the programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF budget, taking into consideration the objectives set forth by the provisions of RA 7160 and RA 5447.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following measures be adopted to ensure effective GAD mainstreaming in the LGU:
Prepare GAD Plan and Budget as well as GAD Accomplishment Reports with their corresponding HGDG checklist to be submitted to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) for consolidation and submission to DILG Regional Office for approval.
Undertake efforts to establish GAD Database which are the basis for gender responsive planning, budgeting and policy formation.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountantto exert more efforts in submitting to COA the lacking financial reports/journals and supporting documents and strictly observe the mandatory deadlines in the submission of reports and supporting documents as mandated under P.D. 1445, NGAS Manual and COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set up and maintain subsidiary ledgers for all receipts and collections of each collecting officer of the LGU and reconcile the book balances with cashbooks of all AOs on a quarterly basis as mandated in the above-mentioned provisions.

We recommended that the accountable officers through the supervision of the Municipal Treasurer revisit and strictly adhere to the accounting policies and procedures on collections and deposits of the NGAS Manual particularly on the timely remittance and reporting of collections in order to safeguard and prevent possible misappropriation or misuse of government funds. Also, adjustments are required for the (over) under recording of collections in order to provide accurate entries in the cashbooks.
observations.reason: During the cash examination of some AOs on November 4, 2016, the Audit Team still noted delays in the remittances and reporting of collections. The Municipal Treasurer justified that due to the distance of the AGDB from their LGU, a few of the collectors on the field and other pressing concerns of her office and that of her office. The Municipal Treasurer assured that all collections for the week were turned over to her by the collectors and deposited before weekend.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the following suggested measures be maintained to ensure a strong internal control system over cash advances: The Accountant shall not allow additional cash advances to any officials and employees unless the previous cash advance is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made; The Accountable Officer shall refund any unexpended balance of her cash advance at the end of each year; The AO shall update the cashbook entries immediately at the time the transaction occurs; The AO shall reconcile the cashbook balance with the accounting records at least every quarter and take up necessary corrections in the books requiring adjustments to reconcile the cashbook and ledger balances; The Accountant shall set up separate subsidiary ledger for each purpose of cash advance under the Trust Fund, and further set up individual subsidiary ledgers for each Accountable Officer making the necessary cash advances to determine the correct accountability in a given period and to heed for possible mistakes in the recording of liquidations from one AO to another; and The head of the agency shall increase the maximum accountability of the above-mentioned accountable officers.

It was recommended that management continue with their efforts in the imposition of liquidation and/or settlement of outstanding cash advances as well as their strict granting and utilization of the same in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 22, 2016.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the: Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to register the real properties and estates of the LGU under the Torens Title System; and Municipal Accountant to secure certified true copies of the TCTs, which shall be used as reference in the recording of existing properties and the properties that shall be acquired in the future operations of the LGU.

We recommended that the accountable officers, inventory committee and accounting unit closely coordinate to complete the reconciliation of their respective records and subsequently submit the reconciliation report to the Office of the Auditor for review and verification. It is further recommended that the Inventory Committee and Accounting Unit continuously conduct the physical inventory-taking and reconciliation of their records to address discrepancies that may arise in the regular course of the agency’s operations, as well as in accordance with Section C.3, Chapter V of the Manual on Property Custodianship.

We recommended that the LGU and Employees’ Association come up with concrete plan/procedures to document and clearly identify the savings generated from the cost-cutting measures and systems improvement undertaken by the agency, otherwise, this will be disallowed in audit. Likewise, savings utilized for the grant of CNA must be taken from allowable MOOE clearly identified in the Agreement.

We recommended that the Accountant should check and verify the completeness of supporting documents attached to the voucher. Management should provide specific duties and responsibilities of casuals, job orders and contractual employees. Accomplishment reports should likewise be submitted by concerned employees before payment of salaries is made.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan spearheaded by the SB Chair on the Committee on Women, Children and Family and assisted by the LGU GFPS hasten the formulation of the GAD Code to guide themselves in establishing policies, plans and programs that would effectively address gender-related issues of the LGU and its constituents.
